title: Chapter 18
text: Chapter Fifteen O nly the merest hint of orange was visible on the horizon when Ronin reached the western guard post. The gearheads, like their counterparts at the east barricade, told him Warlord didn’t appreciate visitors after dark. Ronin calmly pointed out the lingering sunlight. After staring at it for some time, the gearheads exchanged a glance with one another and waved him through . Though his pack was heavy with trade, Ronin didn’t stop in the Market. He was urged on by a different sort of weight, perceived not by his sensors, but by his processors. He had to tell Lara. There was no way to know how she’d react . Only that it wouldn’t be good . The guards at the interior gate gave him no trouble, and he hurried along the lonely stretch of road around the park. Briefly, he entertained the idea of cutting through the park to get home faster, as though surrounding himself with life would make the news easier to deliver . His memory served up the journal entry about people executed and burned in the park, and he decided to stick to the streets . As he walked, questions stacked in his mind, caught in a queue that would never clear. Why? Why, after all the senseless death and destruction the world had already faced, did there have to be more ? What could Tabitha have done to deserve it? Did anything warrant such an end ? Clearly, Warlord had a history of hostility toward humans, but why another bot, this time? The synth hadn’t merely been powered down, he’d been ripped to pieces . There was a message in the act, and though Ronin possessed the necessary information to decipher it, but meaning would not come. His processors insisted there was some sort of logical thought behind the death, deactivation, desecration, and dismemberment. But, despite his own bouts of illogical behavior lately, he couldn’t understand this act. Couldn’t derive a meaningful purpose for it . Too soon, he arrived at his residence. The windows were dark. His mild relief was accompanied by a touch of shame; if Lara was asleep, it would give him more time to determine how to relay the news to her . Ronin removed his gloves, goggles, and mask, stuffing them away, and fished the key out of his pocket. Unlocking the door, he slipped inside . His optics initialized night-vision automatically as he closed the door behind him, but something about using it seemed wrong. He overrode the change and switched on the overhead lights instead . He set his rifle down against the wall, and was shrugging off his rucksack when he noticed Lara on his periphery and looked up . She sat on the third step from the bottom. The barrel of the pistol in her hand was a gaping, black maw, swaying with the trembling of her arm . “I’m so pissed at you right now, I should shoot you,” she said, voice as unsteady as her hand. “Where do you get off leaving without a word ?” There was nothing stopping her from pulling the trigger. Though improbable, there was even a chance that she’d hit him in the optics and do considerable damage. Would it make her feel better ? “I didn’t want to say goodbye. It implies a finality that didn’t seem appropriate .” With a heavy, shaky sigh, she lowered the gun, pointing the barrel at the floor. “What are you doing to me? I can’t even stay mad at you.” She averted her gaze, head bowed . “That because I’m back a day early ?” She smirked and met his optics. “Maybe. Couldn’t stay away , huh ?” Ronin couldn’t identify the gleam in her eyes, but it added vibrancy to her face . “Apparently not,” he replied. The truth of it struck him, hard; he couldn’t stay away from her. After arguing over the length of his trip, claiming three days was barely enough, he’d been the one to cut it short . He dropped his pack on the floor beside the rifle. It landed with a heavy, metallic clang, and a small cloud of dust rose from the fabric . “You weren’t kidding when you said the Dust stays with you .” “It isn’t the only thing in the world, but it permeates everything .” “I still don’t know what the hell you’re talking about most of the time,” she said, a corner of her mouth lifting. Teasing . Was that the right word? “Go on and clean yourself up .” “ Lara — ” “ Not yet .” “I have to — ” “Go clean up, and then you can tell me all the details. I’ve been bored out of my fucking skull since you left, and now my fingertips are sore, on top of it .” She rose lithely, stretched, and walked up the stairs. Ronin watched the gentle sway of her hips from below. He should demand she stop and listen to him . Instead, he followed her up. She strolled into his room, directly toward his bed, and Ronin froze. What was she doing? Why, given what he needed to tell her, was his desire for her amplifying ? Lara stopped in front of the chest, set the pistol down, and returned to the hallway. “It’s not very good, but I made you something. It’s on your bed.” She raised her fingers to show him the tiny cuts on their tips. “I bled for it, so you’d better at least pretend to appreciate it .” He turned his head as she walked past him, following her progress toward her room, unable to form a coherent sentence. Why couldn’t he just tell her ? Death was part of the world; it always had been, and always would be. How many people, whether flesh or metal, had he seen, stilled forever? It was the wrong question to ask himself . Tabitha and the synth made 115,299, at least since his awakening. Many of them were already dead or deactivated long before Ronin found them, but they remained in his memory, constant reminders of the world’s unforgiving nature . He knew there were more — millions of them — buried in the Dust, hidden in the ruins. He knew there were more, from before the Black Out, trapped in his inaccessible memories . Entering his bedroom, he turned on the light and undressed. His fingers were stiff as he worked the zippers and buttons, and his optics wandered — first to the pistol on the chest, and then to the bed. The blankets were rumpled, as though Lara had been atop them while he was gone. Spread atop one of the pillows was a shirt, made from the gray fabric he’d given her before his departure . Ronin brushed his hand over the dimples in the bedding. What had she looked like in his bed? How might it have felt, to be in it with her? His processors could composite her image into the scene, but such could never compare to reality . No; not now, not while he was covered in Dust and carrying such devastating news . He stepped into the adjoined bathroom and showered, using a cloth to wipe away the dirt from his skin. Steam billowed around him as he scrubbed beneath his finger nails. His sensors registered the water temperature — one hundred and ten degrees Fahrenheit — but it was just another number. A few weeks ago, Tabitha would’ve been just another number, too. One hundred and fifteen thousand, two hundred ninety-eight. That was before Lara . Lara was the key. The reason Tabitha was more than a number, more than a nameless face. Because of Lara, Tabitha was a person, and she had loved someone and been loved in return. Her death meant something . Every death did, whether he understood it or not . Turning off the water, he stepped out of the tub, dabbing moisture from his skin with a towel. He stopped in front of the mirror and wiped away the condensation. His face was unchanged. No indication of grief, no sign of the hardships the Dust had wrought upon him . He walked into the bedroom and pulled on a clean pair of pants before picking up the shirt Lara had made; it sported inconsistent stitching, uneven cuts, and a tiny stain near the left shoulder that was likely her blood. Somehow, those flaws made the shirt more appealing . “So, tell me,” Lara said . Ronin turned his head to see her walk in through the open door. Her eyes ran over his bare torso before focusing on his optics. She sat on the edge of the bed . “Your bag looked full. You find anything good ?” “I did,” he replied; he hated that it was the truth. “What’s in the bag doesn’t matter , Lara .” “So…don’t keep me in suspense! Tell me. I don’t think I’ve ever seen you so serious, which says a lot. Not like you had much of a sense of humor to begin with .” He folded the shirt and laid it atop the chest, moving to stand in front of her. She raised her gaze when he crouched to her eye- level . “You’re making this kinda weird, Ronin .” “I found Tabitha .” The words hung in the air, suspended like the ever-present haze in the sky . “What? Where is she? Is she okay? Did you talk — ” He raised his hands, signaling her to stop. She did, though the excitement didn’t fade from her expression. He ignored the electric tingle on his cheek . “She’s dead , Lara .” The color drained from her face, and she stared at him blankly. “That’s a fucking lie .” “I found her on the way into town, with a dismantled synth .” “You’re lying!” she yelled, jumping to her feet. She slapped him, sending a brief, dull throb of pain through his left shoulder . He rose and caught her arms before she struck him again. Lara struggled against his hold as he took both her wrists into one hand, clamping them together . “You’re a lying can of shit! All you fucking bots can go fuck each other and rust that way !” “She had a scar,” he said gently. With one finger, he traced a line from the first knuckle of her left pinky to her thumb . Lara gave a final, surprisingly strong tug on her arms, and then crumpled. Her wail filled his audio receptors. She sagged against him, sobbing against his chest . Ronin released her wrists and — though he wasn’t sure why — wrapped his arms around her. It wasn’t a tight embrace, but it was solid, and she leaned into him. Her body shook, her teardrops tracing wet paths down his abdomen . Didn’t she want to know how? Hadn’t she wanted all the details ? No . That information would only cause more pain. It was enough for her, now, that Tabitha was dead. The cause wasn’t important, the circumstances couldn’t alleviate the grief . “I buried her.” He brushed a palm over her soft hair. “West of town. Away from… all this .” Though she didn’t speak, her sobs quieted. Her breathing was erratic; sharp, shuddering inhalations rocked her body with no predictable pattern . “Now, Tabitha is free,” he continued. “Free from the struggles we all suffer every day. She doesn’t have to worry about…where her next meal will come from, or if she’ll have a roof over her head, or whether a cut will get infected. She can just … rest .” Lara pulled back and looked up at him. Her eyes were red, her cheeks wet, her gaze searching. It reminded him so much of that day in the rain; she’d been lost, nearly broken, but not yet defeated . She leaned forward, not looking away, and pressed her mouth to his . Ronin first registered the warmth of her lips, and then their softness. They yielded to shape of his own, molding to a perfect fit. An electric pulse, unlike anything he’d experienced, spread across his face. This was no malfunction, not a result of an error in his coding . His eyes widened as the sensation crackled over every electrode in his body. His attraction for her, his arousal, surged with new power, triggering the automated systems that pumped fluid into his phallus. It swelled in his pants . Pulling back again, Lara glanced at his lips. Her pink tongue slipped out of her mouth for an instant before she kissed him once more . * * * L ara closed her eyes, waiting for the wave of revulsion, for the surge of panic when she realized that she was kissing a bot . It didn’t come . His lips were surprisingly warm and not nearly as rigid as she thought they’d be. He slid his arms around her, caging her against his chest. Her heart raced, and her breath was short, but it wasn’t because of fear. It was because of his nearness, his touch . Reluctantly, she broke contact again, tilting her head back to study his face. This wasn’t Warlord, wasn’t a random bot. He was Ronin; patient, considerate, trustworthy Ronin . Lara didn’t want to think about what her sister had suffered, didn’t want to feel the heartbreak of losing the only person she’d ever loved. Her life had been pain for as long as she could remember; the pain of hunger, of loneliness, of never being good enough to deserve all that Tabitha had done for her. It gathered in her now, twenty-three years of struggle and uncertainty, writhing in her stomach and constricting her chest . Scattered over time, she could handle it. She was strong enough for that. But all that suffering, balled together, would break her. Beneath the weight of her despair, she wanted to live. She just needed to escape before it crushed her…needed the feelings only Ronin could instill in her. Needed to feel safe. Cared for . Loved ? Anything but acknowledge the reality; she was now truly alone . “Make me forget,” she whispered, lip quivering. “I can’t… I don’t want to think. Not now .” Ronin didn’t hesitate. He lifted her like she weighed nothing, covering her mouth in a kiss that stole her breath even though he didn’t have lungs, and laid her on the bed. It creaked as he climbed atop it and settled between her thighs, propping himself up on an arm. The dizzying speed of his movement stopped as abruptly as it had begun, and his touch became a sensual caress . He gently wiped the moisture from her face, and then trailed his hand along the curve of her jaw, following it to the slender column of her neck. His hand moved down until his fingers brushed the collar of her shirt. Heat blossomed everywhere his skin met hers, pulsing with each beat of her heart . She placed her palms on his chest. It was the first time she’d touched him — if she didn’t count the numerous times she’d hit him — and she was surprised by the feel of his skin. It looked like a human’s in most light, and had the same warmth, the same give, but seemed thicker and not quite as smooth. She felt hints of the metal plates beneath. Though shaped nothing like a human skeleton, they reminded her of bones, nonetheless. Running her hands over his broad shoulders and up his neck, she took hold of his face . Their lips separated, and he gazed down at her . His expression wasn’t blank now. She had no doubt the emotion in it was real. Jaw clenched, lips pressed thin, and body rigid, he was holding himself in check. Awaiting her rejection. The blanket rasped softly as he bunched the fabric in his fist . How many times had she thrown it in his face that he was just a machine? That he was incapable of emotion? She’d been wrong. Terribly wrong. Ronin had proven it time and again, and she’d been too much of a coward to acknowledge it. He felt as deeply, strongly, and passionately as any human she’d known . She brushed her thumbs over his cheeks, and then his brows to smooth the crease between them. From this close, the subtle shades of green woven into his eyes were clear, adding new layers to their unique color . Lara saw him the same way he saw her . Alive . Guiding his face down to hers, she kissed him again. The tightness in his lips vanished when she ran her tongue over them. His mouth opened, and she imagined the gasp he might have released, were he able to breathe. He shifted his hips subtly, pressing his pelvis against her. Lara did gasp as he grinded his bulge against her center . Releasing him, she grasped the hem of her shirt and pulled it over her head. It obstructed her vision for a moment, and when she saw him again, Ronin was staring at her chest. She tossed the garment aside and settled her arms on the bedding to either side of her head. This was the same stare he wore when he watched her dance. Not long ago, it had infuriated her; she knew better now . What was he thinking, when he got that look ? Was he watching her reactions? Though he didn’t touch her again, her body responded to the anticipation of more contact, screaming inwardly for his hands to run over it. Heat pooled at her core and her skin tingled with need . “ Ronin .” He met her eyes . “I won’t break.” She grabbed his wrist and forced his hand down. “ Touch me .” Something changed in his gaze, and like someone flipped a light switch inside him, Ronin turned on . He kissed her lips, her cheek, her jaw and neck, her shoulder . She closed her eyes, arching into his touch, and undulated her hips to feel his hard length through their pants. His hand claimed her other breast, and with each of his caresses, she gave a little more of herself into his keeping . The sound of ripping fabric was thunderous, matched only by the beating of her heart; he’d tore them like they were made of paper . Ronin leaned back, pulling the halves of her pants down to expose her thighs. He stared down at her sex, pupils dilating and shrinking. Lara gasped and lifted her hips, grinding against his hand. He paused for a moment, thoughtful, and before she could question his hesitation, he slipped another finger in . A spark ignited under the sweet warmth of his hand, blazing a path to every nerve ending in Lara’s body. His fingers curled inside of her, brushing sensitive flesh, and fanned the flames into an inferno . She forced her eyes open to look at him. He was still watching her, just as intently as he did when she danced . This time, she’d dance with him, not for him . Taking hold of his wrist, she guided his hand away and sat up. Confusion flickered over his features, but he moved without objection, sitting back. The bed creaked under his weight . Lara kicked off her ruined pants and climbed onto his lap. Ronin’s hands settled on her hips, holding her steady as she wrapped her legs around his waist . Their eyes were level in the new position. They stared at one another, and, for once, she knew exactly what he was thinking. This was the point of no return . And Lara didn’t care . She wanted him. She’d wanted him before tonight, but had shoved her desires aside. Anger was easier than lust, easier than caring. It didn’t matter if it was directed at him, or her feelings, or herself . Not anymore . Their mouths met, Ronin’s hunger equaling her own, their mutual need intensifying with each taste. She ran her fingers through his short hair and down his back, looping her arms around his neck and lifting her ass. The tip of his cock pressed to her center. To delay any further would welcome the pain back. She didn’t want to think. She wanted oblivion . Before another thought crossed her mind, before she could hesitate and convince herself to stop, she dropped down upon him . She exhaled sharply at the sudden fullness. This was nothing like the pain she remembered, nothing like she’d made herself imagine. The discomfort was fleeting, and her body stretched to accommodate him, drawing him deeper inside — hungry for more . Lara buried her face against his neck. Whatever memories she’d feared would make themselves known did not arise. There was only herself and Ronin, in this room, shut away from the rest of the world. Connected in the most intimate way . He was completely still beneath her, fingertips digging into her hips . “Are you all right?” he asked, voice tight . “I’m ready,” she replied between shuddering breaths. There’d be pain, it was unavoidable. Life was pain, and what was sex, if not part of life? But Ronin didn’t want to hurt her. That meant something. That would make it bearable. It was this closeness, this intimacy that she needed. “Find your release .” His fingers slipped into her hair, and he gently tilted her head back, meeting her gaze . “This isn’t about my release.” He kissed her, one arm braced against her back in support, and moved his hips. Like so much about him, it was subtle. Small enough that, at first, she didn’t realize it was happening . The pressure within her grew as his shaft slid in and out. She held her breath, waiting for the searing pain, but there was only the smooth glide of his cock, a little deeper with each thrust. He struck a hidden place inside she hadn’t known existed . They lay together in silence, Ronin’s fingertips lightly running along her outer thigh, up to her hips, and back down . His gentleness was her undoing; reality surged back. Her throat tightened and tears spilled from her eyes. She’d treated him terribly, while he’d gone out of his way time and again to provide for her comfort. His thoughtfulness in burying Tabitha was proof than he could act with compassion. And he’d done it for Lara . There was a painful tightness in her chest, but she forced the words out. “I didn’t mean the things I said, Ronin .” The motion of his hand stopped .
